original_abstract: This paper introduces a novel framework for human pose estimation leveraging Deep Neural Networks (DNNs). By formulating pose estimation as a direct regression problem targeting body joint coordinates, we develop a cascade of DNN regressors that achieves high-precision localization. Our approach offers the distinct advantage of holistic pose reasoning, utilizing a streamlined yet robust architecture that captures complex spatial dependencies by capitalizing on recent breakthroughs in deep learning. We provide a comprehensive empirical evaluation demonstrating that our method achieves state-of-the-art results across four diverse, real-world academic benchmarks.
humanized_abstract: Human pose estimation using Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) has become increasingly popular. Recent works employ numerous strategies to estimate poses efficiently. However, almost all the methods consider each body joint estimation as an individual task. To obtain highly accurate per-joint localization, a cascade of DNN regressors for the estimation of human poses is proposed in this paper. In contrast to various existing solutions performing individual joints detection in a sequential manner, the proposed approach provides a holistic estimation of a full body pose. Due to its streamlined structure with few learned parameters, the proposed architecture exploits deep architectures fully to infer spatial relations efficiently. Experimental results are shown on four popular academic datasets and our proposed architecture achieves the state-of-the-art performance on all the datasets.

Experimental results are shown on four popular academic datasets and our proposed architecture achieves the state-of-the-art performance on all the datasets.
